name: Modal
what_it_does: Serverless cloud compute platform for running Python functions on demand with GPU acceleration, enabling ML inference, training, and data processing without infrastructure management.
use_cases: Serverless GPU inference for custom ML models without managing GPU servers
Burst processing for AI workloads that need compute on demand
Running data pipelines and batch jobs with auto-scaling GPU workers
Hosting custom ML model inference APIs with automatic scaling
Fine-tuning and training ML models on ephemeral GPU clusters
not_for: Always-on latency-sensitive services (Modal has cold start overhead)
Simple CPU-bound tasks where cheaper compute suffices
best_when: You need serverless GPU compute for Python ML workloads without the DevOps overhead of managing CUDA environments, Docker, or Kubernetes.
avoid_when: You need always-on low-latency inference, non-Python runtimes, or predictable fixed costs.

auth.notes: Modal token (ID + secret) for CLI and SDK authentication. Tokens set via `modal token set` or environment variables. No web API — interaction is via the Python SDK.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $30/month free compute credits on free tier
pricing.notes: Per-second billing for actual compute time. Free tier includes $30 monthly credits. Very cost-effective for bursty workloads. No minimum commitment.

performance.rate_limits: No explicit rate limits; queued execution based on available GPU capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python exceptions with clear messages. Container build failures show detailed logs. Runtime errors include stack traces. Modal logs are accessible for debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Function calls are stateless and not inherently idempotent. Applications must manage idempotency at the application level.
known_agent_gotchas: Cold start latency: first invocation after idle period requires container spin-up (seconds to minutes)
Modal functions must be decorated with @app.function — standard Python functions cannot be called remotely
GPU container images take time to build on first deployment — cache container images for faster startup
Modal is Python-only SDK — no REST API for arbitrary language access
Persistent volumes are required for model weights that should not be re-downloaded per call
Secrets must be created in Modal workspace — cannot use local environment variables directly in remote execution
